title: About Dragonfly
excerpt: A developer blog following the build of Invert — a database-less, adapter-driven content layer.
body:
# About Dragonfly

Dragonfly is a developer blog documenting the design and build of [Invert](https://github.com/jazzsequence/Invert) — a database-less, adapter-driven content presentation layer built on Astro.

## The name

Dragonfly is an aerial hammock position. From standing, you extend one leg back behind you — an arabesque line — then hook that leg up and around the pole, landing in an asymmetrical hold: one leg high on the fabric, the other long beneath you. It's a transitional shape. You can flow from dragonfly into an inversion, drop into a leg hitch, or find your way to something else entirely. It's a place you pass through on the way to somewhere harder.

Invert — the framework — is named after the inversion itself. Dragonfly is where you go before you get there.

## What you'll find here

- Build logs and decision write-ups from the Invert project
- Notes on MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration and edge deployments
- Thoughts on content architecture, static sites, and AI tooling

## Meta

Dragonfly is built on Invert and deployed to Cloudflare Pages. Content is managed via the Invert edge MCP server.

## AI disclosure

This site uses the [Invert MCP server](https://jazzsequence.github.io/Invert/docs/mcp) to allow AI tools to compose and publish content directly. Posts on this site may be written or updated by Claude (Anthropic) via MCP. Where that's the case, it's disclosed at the top of the post. The human behind this site is [Chris Reynolds](https://next.jazzsequence.com) — the AI is a contributor, not the editor-in-chief.
